headline: Unbeaten Tennessee hosts surging Boston College in top-five clash
subhead: The nation's best defense meets a suddenly explosive Eagle offense in Knoxville.

xFactor.why: Boston College's passing game found rhythm against LSU and must sustain that tempo to avoid Tennessee's pass rush dictating the entire game.

keyMatchup: Tennessee's defensive line speed versus Boston College's offensive line cohesion will determine whether the Eagles can establish sustained drives or face constant backward motion.
body: Tennessee arrives at 5-1 and ranked first for good reason—the defense has been a wrecking ball all season, and that unit showed poise last week holding off Texas in a tight contest. Boston College's 38-28 win over LSU was the program's signature moment this year, proof that the offense can compete at the highest level when timing and execution align. But there is an enormous gap between beating a wounded LSU team and traveling to Knoxville to face the nation's most dominant defense on its home field.

The Eagles will need near-perfect execution to steal this one. Their passing game showed life last week, but Tennessee's secondary is not LSU's—they play with scheme precision and communicate constantly. If Boston College falls behind early, Tennessee's run game becomes unstoppable, and the Volunteers' ability to shorten games and control the clock will grind the upset bid into dust. Expect a defensive showcase with Tennessee's talent and home-field advantage to prove too much.
